Defendants Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Apr-09-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Attys. GORDON W. RENNEISEN (Tel No. 415-625-5025) and RENEE CALLANTINE (415-230-8737) of Cornerstone Law Group appearing for the Plaintiff;
Attys. EDWARD P. GARSON and DANIEL J. MARSH of Wilson Elser Moskowitz Edelman & Dicker LLP, Tel No. (415) 433-0990 appearing for Defendants Harry Bonn, Bonn Financial and Insurance Services, Metropolitan Life Insurance Company, and New England Life Insurance Company.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling with the sole modification of deleting the last sentence of the tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS HARRY BONN, BONN FINANCIAL AND INSURANCE SERVICES, METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY, AND NEW ENGLAND LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY'S Petition To Compel Arbitration is DENIED. The motion does not establish that the arbitration agreement was presented to plaintiff - he neither signed nor initialed the agreement and does not recall seeing it; defendant Bonn's declaration on this point is conclusory. Under California's law, which applies due to its public policies against adhesive contracts, the agreement is unconscionable and unenforceable on several grounds: (1) it is take-it-or-leave-it boilerplate; (2) the NASD arbitration rules were not attached or otherwise provided; (3) its comparison of "pre-arbitration discovery" with "court proceedings" is misleading; (4) it would effectively bar depositions in a case where they will likely be crucial to ascertainment of the truth; and (5) it seeks to bar claims after six years, though they may be intertwined with newer claims in a pattern of misconduct and not time-barred. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. The Court ordered that Defendants shall have until April 23, 2015 to answer in this case. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/RBU)
